articles.title: Patna School Summer Vacation 2026 Dates: Bihar Government, Private, Army, Airforce & Central Schools Announce Holidays Amid Heatwave & Extreme Weather Conditions; Order Issued by DM Dr Tyagarajan SM - The Sunday Guardian
articles.full_text:
Rising temperatures and severe heatwave conditions across several parts of India have forced administrations to revise school schedules and announce precautionary measures to protect students from extreme summer heat and related health risks during daytime academic activities.
Patna Schools Timing Changed Due to Heatwave
The Patna District Administration has revised school timings because of rising temperatures and severe heatwave conditions across the district. The decision was taken to protect students from extreme heat exposure and related health risks during daytime hours.
The order was issued by District Magistrate Dr Tyagarajan S.M. under Section 163 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS), 2023.
Schools Directed to Follow New Timings
According to the official notification, temperatures in the district have increased significantly, especially during afternoon hours, creating unsafe conditions for students. As a result, restrictions have been imposed on academic activities in all government and private schools. The order also applies to pre-schools and Anganwadi centres.
Under the revised guidelines:
- Classes for students up to Class 5 have been suspended.
- Students of Classes 6 to 8 cannot attend classes after 10:30 AM.
- Schools have been instructed to modify schedules accordingly.
The administration has asked all school authorities to strictly comply with the new timing regulations.
School Summer Holidays for Government Schools Amid Heatwave
Government schools across several districts in Bihar are taking precautionary measures because of the ongoing heatwave. Authorities have either revised school timings or announced early summer vacations to ensure students are not exposed to dangerous afternoon temperatures.
Officials stated that student health and safety remain the highest priority during the extreme weather conditions.
School Summer Holidays for Private Schools Amid Heatwave
Many private schools in Patna and nearby regions have also started adjusting academic schedules in response to the heatwave alerts. Several institutions are expected to begin summer vacations earlier than usual or conduct shorter school hours to reduce heat exposure for children.
School administrations are regularly monitoring weather updates and following district-level instructions.
School Summer Holidays for Central Schools Amid Heatwave
Several central educational institutions, including schools affiliated with national education boards, are also reviewing class timings amid rising temperatures. Schools have been advised to avoid outdoor activities, morning assemblies, and afternoon classes during peak heatwave hours.
Parents have also been requested to ensure students remain hydrated and avoid unnecessary travel during the daytime.
School Summer Holidays for Army Schools Amid Heatwave
Army schools located in heatwave-affected regions are implementing preventive safety measures for students. Authorities are considering revised schedules, reduced outdoor activities, and summer break adjustments depending on local weather conditions.
School officials have been instructed to maintain proper health precautions on campus.
School Summer Holidays for Air Force Schools Amid Heatwave
Air Force schools in several northern and central regions are also monitoring the impact of high temperatures on students. Educational authorities are prioritising student well-being by reviewing academic schedules and taking steps to minimise exposure to extreme heat conditions.
Schools are expected to follow local administration advisories wherever necessary.
Order Effective Till May 26, 2026
The official order came into effect on May 22, 2026, and will remain applicable until May 26, 2026. During this period, schools have also been advised to take necessary precautions to safeguard children from heat-related illnesses.
Authorities emphasised that student safety remains the top priority amid the ongoing heatwave conditions.
Health Concerns Behind the Decision
The administration stated that extreme summer temperatures can severely affect children’s health. Prolonged exposure to intense heat may lead to dehydration, heat exhaustion, dizziness, and other serious medical complications among students.
Since daytime temperatures are reaching dangerous levels, officials considered it necessary to reduce students’ outdoor exposure during school hours.
IMD Heatwave Warning Across Several States
Earlier, the India Meteorological Department issued heatwave alerts for several states, including Bihar, Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, and parts of Central India and Delhi-NCR.
Following these warnings, multiple state administrations across the country have announced changes in school schedules, summer vacations, and safety measures to protect students from extreme weather conditions.

articles.title: पटना में भीषण गर्मी का कहर, बीपी और शुगर अनियंत्रित होने से बढ़ी मरीजों की संख्या - jagran.com
articles.search_term: (लू OR "लू चलना" OR "भीषण गर्मी" OR "प्रचंड गर्मी" OR "कड़ी धूप" OR "तेज धूप" OR "गर्म हवा" OR "उष्ण लहर" OR "ताप लहर" OR "हीट वेव" OR "झुलसाने वाली गर्मी" OR "तपती गर्मी" OR "भयंकर गर्मी" OR "गर्मी का कहर" OR "गर्मी का प्रकोप" OR "अत्यधिक गर्मी" OR मौसम OR "मौसम अपडेट" OR "मौसम का हाल" OR "मौसम पूर्वानुमान" OR "मौसम अलर्ट" OR आईएमडी OR "आईएमडी पूर्वानुमान" OR "आईएमडी अलर्ट" OR "तापमान में बढ़ोतरी" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR "गर्मी से निजात") Bihar
articles.full_text:
पटना में भीषण गर्मी का कहर, बीपी और शुगर अनियंत्रित होने से बढ़ी मरीजों की संख्या
भीषण गर्मी और उमस के कारण पटना में लोगों की सेहत बिगड़ रही है, जिससे ब्लड प्रेशर और शुगर अनियंत्रित हो रहे हैं। ...और पढ़ें
समय कम है?
जानिए मुख्य बातें और खबर का सार एक नजर में
जागरण संवाददाता, पटना। राजधानी में पड़ रही भीषण गर्मी और उमस अब लोगों की सेहत पर भारी पड़ने लगी है। तेज धूप और लगातार पसीना निकलने से बड़ी संख्या में लोग डिहाइड्रेशन की चपेट में आ रहे हैं, इससे ब्लड प्रेशर और शुगर के मरीजों की हालत बिगड़ रही है।
शरीर में पानी और जरूरी मिनरल्स की कमी होने से घबराहट, बेचैनी, सांस लेने में तकलीफ और दिल की धड़कन बढ़ने जैसी समस्याएं तेजी से सामने आ रही हैं। इस कारण राजधानी के पीएमसीएच, आइजीआइएमएस, एनएमसीएच, एम्स, न्यू गार्डिनर रोड अस्पताल में गर्मी से प्रभावित मरीजों की संख्या में वृद्धि दर्ज की गई है।
बीते 24 घंटे में इन शिकायतों के साथ 14 मरीज भर्ती हुए है। इनमें आइजीआइएमएस में तीन, पीएमसीएच में चार, आइजीआइसी में पांच और न्यू गार्डिनर रोड अस्पताल में दो मरीज भर्ती हुआ है। अस्पतालों में डाक्टरों के कक्ष के बाहर मरीजों की लंबी कतारें देखी जा रही हैं।
आइजीआइसी के निदेशक डॉ. सुनील कुमार ने बताया कि गर्मी और लू के कारण शरीर में सोडियम और पोटैशियम का स्तर कम हो रहा है, इससे बीपी और शुगर अचानक अनियंत्रित हो जा रहा है। खासकर बुजुर्ग, मधुमेह, हृदय रोग और उच्च रक्तचाप के मरीज अधिक प्रभावित हो रहे हैं।
खबरें और भी
डॉक्टरों ने लोगों को पर्याप्त मात्रा में पानी पीने, धूप से बचने और खानपान में सावधानी बरतने की सलाह दी है। उन्होंने कहा कि बुखार, कमजोरी, सिरदर्द, चक्कर, उलटी, दस्त, घबराहट या बेचैनी जैसे लक्षण दिखने पर तुरंत चिकित्सकीय सलाह लेनी चाहिए।
बीमारी के प्रमुख लक्षण
इन चीजों से बचने की सलाह
- बाजार में कटे फल और खुले में बिकने वाला गन्ने का जूस
